preconditions: Attacker controls or registers any subdomain under acme.com (e.g. via takeover or dangling DNS).
issues.exploitation: Register evil.acme.com, send the victim an authorize URL with redirect_uri=https://evil.acme.com/cb, capture the access token from the URL fragment. Result: session takeover for any user who clicks the link while signed in.

preconditions: Tokens delivered in URL fragment, exposed to browser history, referer headers, and analytics scripts.
issues.exploitation: Trigger a redirect chain after authorization so the fragment is leaked into a Referer or window.name; or read window.location.hash from a co-resident XSS sink. Result: token theft without needing a code-exchange step.

preconditions: Public client (no client_secret) and an authorization_code flow path is also enabled.
issues.exploitation: Intercept the authorization code (open redirect, mobile app intent hijack, browser extension) and exchange it at /oauth/token without needing a code_verifier. Result: full account takeover.

preconditions: The authorization endpoint accepts admin:read for a public SPA client without consent gating.
issues.exploitation: Phish a normal user with a one-click consent screen that quietly includes admin:read; the issued token grants admin-tier API calls bound to the user's identity. Result: vertical privilege escalation.
Send the victim the authorize URL pointing at the attacker-owned redirect_uri; tokens are delivered straight to the attacker. Result: pre-auth account takeover with one click.

summary: Public SPA client allows wildcard and attacker-owned redirect_uris on the implicit flow with PKCE off — single-click ATO.
